---
title: "ABLE/529 Mastery Tasklist"
format: gfm
author: "Cursor Automation"
date: 2026-03-09
---

## Operating loop (hourly)

1. Read this tasklist and select the highest-value open item.
2. Research with source priority: IRS, SSA, Congress, Treasury/eCFR, then secondary references.
3. Capture findings in QMD blocks (Question -> Model -> Decision) in `10-core-knowledge.qmd`.
4. Log run outcomes and next actions in `99-run-log.qmd`.
5. Re-rank tasks by impact x uncertainty reduction.

## Definition of done for "expert-level"

- Federal ABLE/529 rules mapped with section-level citations.
- High-risk edge cases explained (benefits interactions, recapture, rollovers, excess contributions).
- State-conformity framework created and tested on priority states.
- Practitioner-ready decision checklists and examples maintained.

## Backlog

| ID | Task | Priority | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| T1 | Verify federal ABLE/529 baselines with primary citations (limits, eligibility, tax treatment) | P0 | done | Initial baseline created this run. |
| T2 | Resolve ABLE-to-Work post-2025 legal status with current-year IRS forms/instructions | P0 | in_progress | Potentially high-impact ambiguity. |
| T3 | Build ABLE vs 529 vs 529->ABLE decision matrix by client scenario | P1 | pending | Include SSI/Medicaid and tax lenses. |
| T4 | Create state conformity/recapture tracker template and populate top 10 states | P1 | pending | Focus on deduction states first. |
| T5 | Build citation index: IRC 529, 529A, 4973, SECURE 2.0 sections | P1 | pending | Include plain-language annotations. |
| T6 | Draft practitioner checklist for rollover execution controls/document retention | P2 | pending | Avoid disqualifying mechanics. |

## Next-run execution order

1. T2 (close ABLE-to-Work ambiguity).
2. T5 (hard citation index).
3. T4 (start state tracker with 3-5 states).
